style_definition.color_preference: A palette of Medical Beige, Warm Amber, Terracotta accents, and Charcoal Grey for text.
style_definition.target_audience: Medical experts, review committees, and clinical researchers

information_density.instruction: Maintain clean margins and clear hierarchy. Academic content requires space to breathe; use a balanced ratio of white space to text to ensure readability during high-stakes defense.

grid_system.instruction: Use a 12-column grid to align complex data and text. Encapsulate distinct experimental steps or data points in subtle, rounded cards with soft borders.

data_visualization.instruction: Charts must look rigorous. Use thin lines, clear axis labels, and highlight statistical significance (P < 0.05) with a warm amber glow or callout box.

typography.instruction: Use a clean Modern Sans for body text and data. Use a sophisticated Classic Serif for slide titles and 'Q&A' to convey authority and tradition.

decorations.instruction: Incorporate subtle background textures like a faint DNA helix, cellular patterns, or microscopic grid lines in the background. Use thin line connectors for flowcharts.

artistic_style.instruction: 1. Art Style & Mood: A blend of high-tech laboratory precision with a warm, humanist touch. 2. Materials & Elements: Frosted glass containers, matte paper textures, and organic medical shapes. 3. Light & Space: Soft golden hour sunlight effects hitting glass elements, creating depth through layered transparency.

required_layouts.layout_intent: Full-bleed background image with warm amber lighting. Center the title in a semi-transparent frosted glass container to maintain high contrast.
A clean vertical timeline style list. Use amber dots as progress markers for each chapter of the presentation.
Split layout. Left side for the text-based challenges. Right side should feature the '15.8%' statistic in a large, bold terracotta font inside a focused card.
Horizontal 3-step process. Each step (Sample, Grouping, Detection) is an icon-topped card connected by a thin dotted line.
The chart takes the primary 2/3 of the slide. Use a 'Positive' badge (amber/gold) to highlight the experimental group's breakthrough.
A 2x2 grid representing the 'Pyramid' of logic. Use subtle numbering (1, 2, 3, 4) in the background of each quadrant to show the flow of conclusions.
An elegant closing slide. Large Serif 'Q&A' text centered over a blurred, warm background image of a professional clinical setting.
